title: Buildah copy from another container
body:
**Description**

I want to copy some binary from another container to a container that I'm building using buildah copy

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

I'm following the tutorial and:
```bash
┬─[dgromov@pc
╰─>$ buildah pull docker.io/composer
8f2928e1f548c952d8da891cb25ca2a13fec36585046dadd83e74925510919ab
┬─[dgromov@pc
╰─>$ man buildah copy
┬─[dgromov@pc
╰─>$ man buildah copy
┬─[dgromov@pc
--> $set mnt_composer (buildah mount docker.io/composer)
cannot mount using driver overlay in rootless mode. You need to run it in a `buildah unshare` session
> buildah unshare
Welcome to fish, the friendly interactive shell
Type `help` for instructions on how to use fish
# set mnt_composer (buildah mount docker.io/composer)
error reading build container "docker.io/composer": error reading build container: container not known
# buildah pull docker.io/composer
8f2928e1f548c952d8da891cb25ca2a13fec36585046dadd83e74925510919ab
# set mnt_composer (buildah mount docker.io/composer)
error reading build container "docker.io/composer": error reading build container: container not known
>
```
**Describe the results you received:**
Not an easy way to copy files from another container


**Describe the results you expected:**
being able to do something like:

```Dockerfile
COPY --from=docker.io/composer /usr/bin/composer /usr/bin/composer
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.15.1-1.fc32.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.15.1
Go Version:      go1.14.6
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:
image Version:   5.5.1
Git Commit:
Built:           Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

```
That's a bug also in built date right?


**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Fedora release 32 (Thirty Two)
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="32 (Workstation Edition)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=32
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f32"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 32 (Workstation Edition)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;34"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:32"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora/f32/system-administrators-guide/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=32
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=32
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="Workstation Edition"
VARIANT_ID=workstation
Fedora release 32 (Thirty Two)
Fedora release 32 (Thirty Two)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux pc 5.7.17-200.fc32.x86_64 #1 SMP Fri Aug 21 15:23:46 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# This file is is the configuration file for all tools
# that use the containers/storage library.
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information
# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"


[storage.options]
# Storage options to be passed to underlying storage drivers

# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

```

comments.body: I figure out I was trying to copy from an image instead of a container, but is there any way I can copy files from an image into a container ? Thanks!

@Davidnet does something like this in your Containerfile work for you?

```
FROM haskell:8.6 AS builder
WORKDIR /opt/laser-egg

COPY --from=builder /root/.local/bin/laser-egg /usr/bin/laser-egg
```
Yes, but I was hoping to do everything in a bash file to not have a Dockerfile

Ah, then you need the Buildah command-line commands.  Something like this me thinks:

```
# alpinectr=$(buildah from alpine)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob df20fa9351a1 done  
Copying config a24bb40132 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

# busyboxctr=$(buildah from busybox)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 9c075fe2c773 done  
Copying config edabd79595 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

# alpinemnt=$(buildah mount $alpinectr)
# touch $alpinemnt/tom.txt
# echo "Tom was here" >> $alpinemnt/tom.txt
# cat !$
cat $alpinemnt/tom.txt
Tom was here

# busyboxmnt=$(buildah mount $busyboxctr)
# cp $alpinemnt/tom.txt $busyboxmnt
# cat $busyboxmnt/tom.txt
Tom was here

# buildah commit $busyboxmnt tombusybox
error reading build container "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/4fea56372f1550a0960bd106497f70132e39bfac91bea6f7405704b99316357e/merged": error reading build container: container not known

# buildah commit $busyboxctr tombusyboxctr
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob c632c18da752 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob a72b99457ace done  
Copying config 61d4388c01 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
61d4388c01e7590ba4fa5b829148aaf7c7134d859d7252e2e73d8ec45345c2eb

# buildah from tombusyboxctr
tombusyboxctr-working-container


# buildah run tombusyboxctr-working-container ls
bin      dev      etc      home     proc     root     run      sys      tmp      tom.txt  usr      var
# buildah run tombusyboxctr-working-container cat tom.txt
Tom was here

```
I'll try it, but of course is more verbose, do you think that this could merit a feature request?
We're always hip for a feature request.  Just create an issue with "RFE: " in the title please.
and fwiw, the example above is a bit fluffy.  I added a few commands to illustrate progress....
Thanks! This really helps me, @TomSweeneyRedHat  awesome work, closing for now.

I could envision a buildah copy extension, where you could say

buildah copy CTR1 /etc/shadow CTR2:/etc/

Which would do what you want.

Podman cp has syntax similar to this.

 podman cp [options] [container:]src_path [container:]dest_path
